purpose: Top-level reference for Synapse's !{command} invocation system. Explains the three-tier command resolution hierarchy, path placeholders ({tracker_root}/{project_root}), categorized command groups (Swarm Lifecycle, Monitoring, Server Control, Project Setup, Analysis, TOC, PKI, Audit, Discovery), how command files work, and dashboard resolution semantics. Includes a quick-reference table of every command grouped by category.
gotchas: Command files are read fresh on every invocation -- the agent does not 'remember' command behavior
Project root resolution priority: --project flag > .synapse/project.json > CWD
Agents have a fixed DASHBOARD ID directive in their system prompt -- no auto-detection or scanning
patterns: Command files are complete specifications -- agents follow them exactly without improvising
Commands organized by functional groups (Swarm Lifecycle, Monitoring, Setup, Analysis, etc.)
conventions: First-match-wins resolution starting from highest-priority Synapse commands
Dashboard arguments default to assigned dashboard, override with --dashboard {id}
